items.md: # THE FIRST FULL-LENGTH CRITICAL STUDY OF THE GENIUS WHO CREATED DUCKBURG AND UNCLE SCROOGE
items.value: THE FIRST FULL-LENGTH CRITICAL STUDY OF THE GENIUS WHO CREATED DUCKBURG AND UNCLE SCROOGE

items.md: “A lifelong devotion to Carl Barks and the Disney comic results in a meticulously and bravely researched chronicle of the great Duck Man’s efforts, viewed in a critical and holistic vein. Barks is here established, finally, as a giant of twentieth-century popular culture, in the context of his times: Cold War, consumerism, ecology, technology, questions of gender and social hierarchy, U.S. imperialism, and the impact of Scrooge-ish greed on the Third World. The conservative Barks is shown as a radical satirist.”
items.value: “A lifelong devotion to Carl Barks and the Disney comic results in a meticulously and bravely researched chronicle of the great Duck Man’s efforts, viewed in a critical and holistic vein. items.md: Author of *The History of the Comic Strip* and *Che Guevara: Icon, Myth, and Message*
items.value: Author of The History of the Comic Strip and Che Guevara: Icon, Myth, and Message

items.md: THOMAS ANDRAE, an instructor in the cinema department of San Francisco State University, is the senior editor and cofounder of *Discourse: Journal for Theoretical Studies in Media and Culture*. He produced *The Duck Man*, a feature-length documentary on Carl Barks, and was an editor of the Carl Barks Library.
